claims.summary: High-mode telemetry may lose nuance while crossing the WebSocket and BridgeState boundary.
claims.disposition: Verified the parsed full SpectralTelemetry value is stored and assigned directly; no bridge-side eigenvalue quantization or smoothing occurs.

claims.summary: The latency from connection to the first valid spectral packet should be measured.
claims.disposition: Verified connection-scoped first-valid packet time, lag, entropy, and reconnect reset are already recorded and tested.

claims.summary: Slow BridgeState updates may create temporal mismatch or perceptual stutter.
claims.disposition: Verified TelemetryIntegrationHealthV1 separates prewrite processing, lock wait, and lock hold so integration delay can be localized without inferring felt causation.

claims.summary: A jitter buffer or moving average should smooth lambda distributions before state replacement.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because smoothing changes live sensory semantics, temporal fidelity, and the bridge-to-Minime contract.
